roadmap: URGENT: this finding scores in the critical tier under the selected threat profile. Treat as a top-priority migration.
Enable hybrid post-quantum TLS key exchange on the server (X25519MLKEM768 is the IETF-standardized choice as of 2026).
Pair hybrid KEX with a hybrid Ed25519+ML-DSA-65 certificate chain when the upstream CA supports it; until then the existing classical certificate is acceptable as long as KEX is hybrid.
Disable TLS 1.0/1.1 if still enabled; the hybrid groups require TLS 1.3.
Audit cipher-suite policy: keep AES-GCM and ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD; remove RSA key-exchange suites entirely.
MODERATE: include in the next planned configuration review.
Hybrid PQ key exchange is in place. Verify the deployed group matches the IETF-standardized X25519MLKEM768 (RFC 9794) rather than an early experimental hybrid that may be deprecated.
Track the IETF roadmap for migration to hybrid certificate chains (Ed25519+ML-DSA-65) once your CA offers them.
Continue monitoring for downgrade attempts: hybrid-only policy is preferable to hybrid-preferred-but-classical-fallback.
